countries/china.summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW — GLOBAL CRACKDOWN FEARS (Jul 6): ANI News Jul 6: "China's ethnic unity law raises fear of expanded global crackdown on dissent." Law in force since Jul 1; senior officials at Jun 24 State Council press confirmed extraterritorial applicability. Overseas individuals/organizations can be held liable for "undermining ethnic unity." Amnesty, UN OHCHR, European Parliament condemned law as "legal blueprint for cultural erasure" targeting Uyghurs, Tibetans, Mongolians. CNN Jul 1: "China tells minorities to assimilate." Taiwan government issued formal response. Germany Foreign Ministry expressed "great concern."

countries/el-salvador.summary: ONGOING REPRESSION — DOWNGRADE APPLIED JUL 5 (now 15.0/Critical): Cristosal (El Salvador's leading human rights organization) suspended operations July citing "escalating repression"; Ruth López, Alejandro Henríquez, José Ángel Pérez declared prisoners of conscience by Amnesty International. Constitutional amendment allowing indefinite re-election enacted. Foreign Agents Law: 140+ rights defenders/journalists fled including El Faro staff. 86+ political prisoners; 245+ facing persecution. ICJ classified regime as "crimes against humanity within public security policy." Note: proposed downgrade (20.3→15.0) was APPLIED on Jul 5; baseline is now 15.0.

countries/burkina-faso.summary: SAHEL LEAN SEASON PEAK + AID ACCESS CRISIS (Jul 6, sector sweep): 4.5M people in need of humanitarian assistance (2.9M children). 1.6M require emergency food assistance. 2M+ internally displaced. Junta arrests of aid workers increasing; new laws restricting humanitarian operations. EU allocated €27.5M initial assistance. Burkina Faso among 9.1M at IPC Phase 3+ in Central Sahel lean season (Jun-Aug). FAO/WFP joint briefing Jul 9 will address Sahel situation.

countries/mali.summary: JNIM SIEGE ONGOING + NEW COORDINATED ATTACKS JUL 4: ACLED/Bellingcat Jul analysis: JNIM's fuel blockade is strangling Bamako — security sources say organization is tightening siege, choking critical supplies. Jul 4: coordinated attacks in Anefis, Kenioroba, southwest Mali — marking new stage in southward expansion. JNIM fighters attacked Bourem, Bamako, Kati, Sévaré, Senou, Mopti since April 28 onset. HRW Jun 28 (in window): "grave abuses amid renewed fighting" — civilian executions documented. 5M in humanitarian need; response only 18.5% funded. FAO/WFP Sahel briefing Jul 9 forthcoming.

countries/ethiopia.summary: DROUGHT DISPLACEMENT + NUTRITION GAPS (Jul 6, sector sweep): MSF Afder/Shebelle assessment (in window): urgent gaps in water and nutrition services, local health capacity overstretched. Humanitarian funding shortfalls affecting response.

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: EBOLA BUNDIBUGYO — 1,561 CONFIRMED, 506 DEAD (Jul 6 WHO update): As of Jul 6, 1,561 confirmed cases with 506 deaths — up from 1,406/438 on Jul 3 (averaging 52+ new confirmed per day). Ituri province most affected: 1,417 cases, 424 deaths across 24 of 36 health zones. 628 hospitalised; 254 recovered. International spread: France (1 imported case, Jun 24); Uganda (20 confirmed, contained). UN News Jul 6: healing from stigma on Uganda frontline. No approved vaccine or treatment for Bundibugyo strain. PHEIC active. M23/FARDC ongoing conflict; 7.2M internally displaced.

countries/venezuela.summary: EARTHQUAKE DAY 12 (Jul 6) — TOLL 3,300+: Official and media figures now converge on 3,300+ confirmed dead, 16,400+ injured, 50,000+ unaccounted for. Forensic pathologists warn true toll may be 3× the official figure. UN estimated $4.7–8.7B in direct damage (~4–8% of GDP). 189 buildings completely levelled of 800 collapsed. WFP appealed $50M; UNICEF supplies for 100,000 in-country. Monsoon season risk intensifying. Venezuelan government $200M reconstruction fund covers <1% of estimated need. Ongoing search-and-rescue operations hampered by rubble and displaced infrastructure.

countries/russia.summary: KYIV ATTACKS (Jul 5–6): Russia launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting Kyiv, killing 15+ in the city and 6 in surrounding region. Russia also struck Dnipro (17 killed), Zaporizhzhia and Kherson regions (4 killed, 37 wounded). July 2026 civilian toll: 170/day average — highest rate since 2023, significantly above 2025. EU Council sanctioned 9 Bucha massacre individuals. EU Special Tribunal for crime of aggression operational. 20,500+ Ukrainian children forcibly deported. EU Russia sanctions extended to July 2027.

countries/yemen.summary: ONGOING HUMANITARIAN CRISIS — FUNDING SHORTFALLS (Jul 6, sector sweep): 10 years of war; 4.5M+ displaced; 18.2M in need of humanitarian assistance and protection. Yemen hosts 61,240 refugees mostly from Somalia/Ethiopia. Medical and humanitarian organizations withdrawing due to funding shortfalls. Rising fuel prices from Middle East conflict further constrain response. Yemen remains one of world's largest humanitarian crises per UNHCR/WFP.

countries/sudan.summary: RSF EL OBEID SIEGE — AL JAZEERA JUL 6: Al Jazeera published Jul 6: "El-Obeid under siege by RSF: Could this be Sudan's next el-Fasher?" — RSF systematically tightening siege. Amnesty International launched "City Under Siege, Children Under Fire" report Jul 1 documenting RSF crimes against humanity in North Darfur. UN Human Rights Council held landmark session Jul 3 to prevent El Obeid atrocities (HRW). HRW Jul 3: RSF drone strikes hit 16+ civilian targets including hospitals, schools, power stations; 45 civilians killed in 15 drone attacks Jun 6–28. Washington Post and UN News covered UN "red alert" (Jul 3). 500,000 civilians + 105,000 IDPs at siege risk.

countries/south-sudan.summary: IPC PHASE 5 FAMINE RISK (ongoing Jul 6): 7.8M at IPC Phase 3+ (56% of population). 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate — in Akobo, Fangak, Nyirol, Uror (Jonglei) and Luakpiny/Nasir, Ulang (Upper Nile). Credible famine risk in four counties. 300,000 displaced in Jonglei; 200,000 from Akobo since March 6. 2.2M children acutely malnourished; 700,000 projected severe acute malnutrition through July. DRC Ebola now in Haut-Uele province (borders South Sudan). Cholera, malaria, measles compounding.

countries/somalia.summary: DEEPENING FOOD CRISIS + DROUGHT (Jul 6, sector sweep): 6.5M Somalis (~25% of population) facing high levels of acute food insecurity. 2M+ at IPC Phase 4 (emergency). 1.8M children under 5 expected to suffer acute malnutrition in 2026. Federal government declared national drought emergency Nov 2025. MSF teams documented urgent gaps in water and nutrition services in Afder and Shebelle zones. UNHCR, WFP, UNICEF, UNFPA all active with 2026 appeals.

countries/afghanistan.summary: PAKISTAN-AFGHANISTAN CONFLICT + CHRONIC CRISIS (Jul 6, sector sweep): ACAPS Jun 10 anticipatory note (in window) documented anticipated humanitarian impacts of Pakistan-Afghanistan conflict — 100,000+ residents in Bargi Matal/Kamdesh lacking humanitarian access; closure of roads due to cross-border fire. Pakistan declared "open war" in February; fragile ceasefire attempts. UN experts called for lasting peace.

countries/haiti.summary: TPS WORK AUTHORIZATIONS EXPIRE JUL 10 — 350K HAITIANS AT RISK: DHS announced TPS work authorizations (Employment Authorization Documents) expire Jul 10, 2026. Supreme Court 6-3 ruling (Jun 25, Mullin v. Doe) cleared Trump administration to terminate TPS for ~350,000 Haitians — they revert to illegal status, face deportation. NPR Jul 2: US healthcare system impact highlighted as Haitian healthcare workers lose authorization. Florida Republican Carlos Gimenez called deportations a "huge mistake." Deportees face kidnapping/extortion risks as gangs control ~90% of Port-au-Prince. 1.5M+ internally displaced; 5.8M (52%) at crisis food insecurity.
